title: mentha × kerneri var. pycnotricha
intro: Mentha × kerneri var. pycnotricha, also known as Cornish mint, is a naturally occurring hybrid of the mint plant species. It is characterized by its dark green, serrated leaves and distinctive purple flowers. Cornish mint has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of menthol and menthone. In herbal preparations, Cornish mint is often used to alleviate digestive issues and reduce stress, and it can be found in teas, infusions, and topical creams.
